item.title: It's hecn warm
item.description:
The last few days have been hecn hot here in NL, I hope you are all staying hydrated!
Cat tax: Ms. Bean! Her favourite spot is behind the canvases on my desk :'D If you would like to see more of my illustrations you can find them on my Instagram (I don't post them here as much as I do there): https://www.instagram.com/revives_illustrations Or twitter: https://twitter.com/RevivesI
item.dc:creator: AmyRevives

item.title: I knit a sweater
item.description:
Pattern is Langlade by Melissa Schaschwary https://www.ravelry.com/patterns/library/langlade
Bird tested bird approved
The sleeves have a nice simple lace pattern for drama
Bonus content Merlin wanted to be in this one
item.dc:creator: avianidiot

item.title: Low octave
item.dc:creator: Savegeone007

item.title: Dollar store trash ship!
item.description:
Hey yall, sorry! Last post glitched out but my girlfriend and I made a boat! It's for a pirate themed one shot we are having! ☠
We call it the Sapphire Serpent and we made it from junk we had lying around and junk from the dollar tree near us!
Collectively it probably cost us under 10 bucks to make!
This balcony removes so we can switch the lights off and on!
Inside the captains quarters!
How it all started! -Edit! I had some people mention it but you can find more of my girls art on Facebook and Instagram under Amber.escobar.art! -A one shot is a single story dnd campaign where we will play for a day then not run it anymore as I'm doing it for someone who's moving away and has never gotten to play -It took us about a week not counting minecraft breaks! -I will make another post later kinda giving the steps of how we made it! -I have some other things we built for it if anyone was interested in that!
item.dc:creator: Pengu3000

item.dc:creator: badesch

item.title: Let’s Go Blue Jay!
item.description:
Adult, spring 2022. These birds were all captured using strict ethical guidelines and with permits, for the purpose of scientific data collection on migratory bird species.
Adult, spring 2022
Adult, spring 2022
Adult, fall 2019
Adult, fall 2019
Adult, fall 2019
Juvenile (hatch-year bird), fall 2019
Juvenile (hatch-year bird), fall 2018. Young birds have grown in some of their adult feathers, but still retain some of their less-vibrant baby feathers! (halfway across wing)
Juvenile (hatch-year), fall 2018. Scruffy baby feathers being replaced gradually by brightly coloured adult feathers
Juvenile (hatch-year), fall 2018
Abnormal tail feather- no white tip, and too long! Fall 2018
Adult, spring 2018. There are no juvenile birds flying in spring, and this bird has all of its’ adult plumage
Adult, spring 2018. I can’t determine the sex of this bird unless I look at the tummy in spring- a female loses a patch of feathers low on her belly, so that she makes direct contact with her eggs to keep them warm.
This guy borrowed a crest feather from a cardinal! Just one abnormally pigmented feather. The little aluminum band on the right leg has a unique number, so that if anyone ever finds it they can find out where it was banded, approximate age, and sex if known.
The tools in the background are special pliers to attach the bands loosely around the leg. Also measure the length of their wing and their weight at the time of banding.
item.dc:creator: nursehoneybadger
